text:
the bitcoin community has argued about quantum security for years and landed nowhere

the reason is simple 

every solution on the table asks bitcoin to change something it was built never to change

→ a hard fork splits the network
→ freezing exposed coins breaks the credibility of self-custody
→ migrating funds to new address types breaks the wallets people already trust

the conservative side of crypto rejected all three, and they were right to

@quipnetwork looked at the same problem and approached it from a completely different direction

instead of changing bitcoin, they built a layer that wraps around it

→ post-quantum protection sits on top of the existing chain
→ your coins never move
→ no fork, no migration, no new address scheme
→ self-custody stays exactly where it is

the protection is real and already running, not a proposal waiting on a vote

→ 20k+ wallets already covered
→ 500+ nodes operating
→ 13k+ active users on the network

quantum breaking bitcoin is a question of when, not if

@quipnetwork is one of the only teams that built the answer before the deadline forced everyone to care

$QUIP is the asset attached to that layer

text:
the next leap in ai isn't bigger models, it's better problems to solve

the bottleneck shifted a while ago and most people are still focused on parameter counts instead of compute architecture

@quipnetwork sits at the part of the stack everyone is going to need next

why ai training hits a wall

training large models means solving optimization problems with billions of parameters

classical computers do this by brute force, billions of small calculations running in parallel for weeks at a time

at some point the energy and time cost stops being worth the marginal gain

quantum hardware approaches the same problems differently, finding optimal configurations across massive search spaces in a fraction of the steps

$QUIP turns that capability into a marketplace anyone can tap into

the robotics problem nobody talks about

robotics needs something ai labs don't, real time decisions in physical environments

path planning, sensor fusion, motion control, grasp optimization, all of it requires solving combinatorial problems on the fly

classical chips inside robots can handle simple cases but break down once the environment gets complex enough

@quipnetwork lets a robot offload those calculations to a quantum compute layer in seconds

the result is robots that can react to messy real world conditions instead of memorized scenarios

what makes the network design different

most quantum compute access today goes through cloud providers with api keys, fiat billing, and waiting queues

none of that scales for ai agents or autonomous robots that need to act independently

$QUIP gives the same access programmatically, no human in the middle, no billing infrastructure to fight with

compute jobs route through a token, not a contract negotiation

why the timing actually matters

ai labs are running into compute walls right now and robotics companies are quietly building toward physical deployment

both need a new layer of compute to keep moving forward

@quipnetwork is the only project building that layer with crypto rails underneath

q2 2026 mainnet timing puts this directly in the window where ai compute scarcity becomes a real bottleneck

the people positioning now are the ones who saw the convergence coming before the rest of the market did

• 3–6s for Mid-Quoters

Strategy

Mode B - Copy: mirror Smart MMs when both align with asymmetric spreads

The Quant Who Turned Shannon's 77-Year-Old Formula Into +$555K in 30 Days

He's not betting on crypto. He's compressing its uncertainty - one bit at a time

His strategy: Shannon → Kelly → Crypto Up/Down

The Formula

D_KL(P ∥ Q) = Σ p(xᵢ) · log₂ [ p(xᵢ) / q(xᵢ) ]

P = his model. Q = the market price. The result = how many bits of information he has that the market doesn't. That number IS the edge

KL > 0.10 bits → Pro zone. Trade

text:
Gemini is now powering the Higgsfield Supercomputer.

The first thing you feel is how much better it handles detailed prompts. You can describe an idea the way you’d actually say it out loud, and the generation stays coherent instead of drifting halfway through.

The motion quality is also really polished. Cinematic feel, consistent visuals, no weird breaks between shots.

The part I keep coming back to is the frame by frame control. Being able to fix one shot without redoing the whole thing changes how you actually work.

Gemini powered search is the quiet upgrade. It picks up on references and niche aesthetics with way more depth, because there’s a much wider base of knowledge behind it.

AI video workflows are starting to feel much closer to real creative production.

text:
Most AI video tools are basically shot generators.

You describe a scene. Generate a clip. Move on.

@TopviewAIhq is going in a different direction with Drama Studio.

You can start with just a prompt, rough idea, or script, and the agent handles the entire workflow from there. Characters, scenes, dialogue, voice, editing. Everything connected together in one workflow.

Turning a story into an actual series used to require funding, a team, or months of production time. Drama Studio compresses that into something one person sitting at a computer can realistically pull off.

Short drama runs on speed and iteration. You need to keep shipping while keeping the story consistent.

That became a lot more accessible.

text:
Most creative teams are still stuck in the single asset workflow. One ad, a few tweaks, then another version from scratch. Repeat that over and over.

The models already got good. The real bottleneck now is the workflow around them.

MAXFUSION just launched Flows, and what actually makes it interesting goes beyond the generation itself. You build a creative pipeline once and reuse it, then run hundreds of variations off the same core idea. The shots stay connected, and the style stays consistent even when you mix different models.

The bigger shift is economic.

When testing 30 ideas suddenly costs almost the same as testing 3, creative teams stop playing safe. You start exploring weirder hooks, sharper concepts, different emotional angles, and ideas that normally wouldn't survive a traditional production process.

That's usually where the best performing ads come from.

text:
A lot of people generate AI videos straight from a prompt, then wonder why the shots, pacing, and motion feel random.

Usually the model’s just trying to make too many decisions at once.
 
I’ve been finding Topview Storyboard pretty useful for this lately.
 
You just give it a reference image and a one-line story idea, and it can generate a cinematic storyboard first, then build the video from it.
 
Way easier than trying to design an entire shot sequence from scratch with prompts.
 
What I like most about this workflow is that it pushes the trial and error before the actual video generation step.
 
Instead of wasting generations on broken outputs, you iterate through lightweight storyboard versions first, which feels much more controllable.
 
They also launched an Ultra Plan with 365 days of unlimited GPT Image 2 + Seedance 2 generations. If you’re running a lot of iterations, the unlimited tier removes the cost anxiety completely.
 
@TopviewAIhq

text:
Step 1: Generate a director-style PREVIS action storyboard in GPT using simple natural language.

This helps reduce motion randomness and makes the action, camera movement, and ink flow feel more intentionally directed.

“Create a director-style PREVIS action storyboard focused on 8 continuous action frames using only minimal stick figures. No realistic anatomy, clothing, pants, shoes, or character details. Show only movement, camera choreography, momentum trails, and ink motion, with only a small amount of necessary text.”

STYLE ink wash minimalism + abstract fashion silhouette + dynamic motion ink + negative space composition + ultra wide perspective distortion + clean high contrast rendering

0:00-0:02: 24mm low-angle close tracking shot. The dancer rapidly shifts weight in groove motion, swinging the brush from behind the body to create the first long ink arc while stepping sideways toward the camera; ink softly spreads along the clothing edges. 0:08-0:10: The camera keeps retreating under pressure. The dancer aggressively advances with three powerful steps, each paired with a large brush swing; layered heavy ink structures rapidly stack, increasing visual density. SFX: (dense groove rhythm, fast brush sweep, continuous motion texture)
0:10-0:12: The camera suddenly pushes close. 0:12-0:13.5: The camera briefly stabilizes while still being pressured backward. 0:13.5-0:15: Extreme frontal wide-perspective close shot. Honored to be invited by @openart_ai to become an OpenArtist

What I like about OpenArt is pretty simple

It doesn’t feel like a tool made only to generate pretty images
more like a place where ideas can move faster, and that matters a lot

AI creation is not about removing the creator
it’s about thinking visually, testing faster, refining as you go
and getting closer to what you actually imagined

The best tools don’t replace taste
they give taste more room to work

Time to make new work
More soon

text:
You don’t need to save too many prompt templates.

GPT Image 2 understands the intent pretty well.
Just give it the animation prompt, then ask for the matching character sheet, key visual, or title design.

After that, the Seedance 2.0 part gets much easier.

Prompt:
SUBJECTS
Pastry Chef: adult, wearing a chef uniform, positioned on the ground level, using a large stainless steel cake spatula to chase along the edge of the countertop, escalating from controlled swings to fully uncontrolled strikes (character reference @ image1)
Cockroach: small-sized, always moving on the countertop, low-position rapid crawling, advancing unidirectionally to the right along the countertop while performing left-right dodges and abrupt stops with direction changes (character reference @ image2)
ENVIRONMENT
Home kitchen, continuous long countertop with clear depth
Common kitchen objects distributed across the countertop (bowls, plates, whisk, measuring cups, etc.)
Window is closed
STYLE
Realistic 3D animation, strong physical feedback
CAMERA DETAILS
POV is a close-following rear perspective behind the cockroach, low angle moving forward along the countertop; attacks primarily press in from above and from the sides, with only one brief frontal confrontation
Timeline
0:00-0:02:
Medium shot, 35mm, slow push-in. The chef stands on the left side of the countertop, lightly whistling while organizing, mouth movement perfectly synchronized with the whistling sound (lip-sync); countertop objects are neatly arranged; the cockroach crawls in the corner making subtle noise, the chef gradually stops and locks onto the target.
SFX: whistling (only present in this phase), subtle crawling
0:02-0:04:
Low-angle tracking shot, 28mm, forward movement. The cockroach accelerates to the right along the countertop and begins dodging; the chef lunges forward and starts striking, the whistling sound completely stops the moment the action begins; the first hit strikes the countertop edge, causing objects to shift from vibration.
SFX: footsteps, heavy metal impact
0:04-0:10:
POV shot, 20mm, close-follow high-speed forward movement. The camera continuously advances without looking back; the cockroach performs serpentine dodging; the chef’s attacks press in from above and the sides, each strike accidentally and realistically hitting objects along the way, gradually creating and maintaining a chaotic state.
SFX: continuous heavy impacts, object collisions
0:10-0:12:
POV slows down. A window appears ahead; the cockroach stops on the countertop inside the window, turns to face the chef directly, shows a smug smile and makes a provocative gesture.
SFX: brief pause
0:12-0:14:
Medium shot push-in. The chef throws the spatula toward the cockroach; after release, no tool remains in hand; the spatula misses and hits the window, shattering the glass; the cockroach continuously jumps out through the broken opening.
SFX: air slice, glass shatter
0:14-0:15:
Wide static shot. The chef’s hands are empty, turns to see the entire countertop in complete chaos (all struck objects remain scattered, overturned, and dirty), brief pause followed by a breakdown scream.
SFX: silence → breakdown scream

text:
The Taking of the Relic

Made with GPT Image 2 + Seedance 2 on @mitte_ai 

This time I started with a storyboard, then moved to video.

The main reason is cost.
Video iterations burn credits really fast. With a storyboard, I can tweak things shot by shot and catch issues early, which is way cheaper.

It also makes the whole process more controllable.
I can adjust each shot on its own, so the character, composition, and key elements stay consistent.

Pacing gets figured out early.
Once the storyboard is done, the flow is pretty much set. The video step is just turning those shots into a sequence.

Sharing my workflow 👇
label: high

text:
Step 1: Generate the storyboard image with GPT Image 2 using the prompt below

Create a single cinematic storyboard image containing 8 panels, arranged in a 4-column horizontal grid layout across the canvas.
Panels are evenly distributed in 4 columns, forming a balanced multi-row composition.
Use generous white space between panels and around the entire layout, creating a calm, refined editorial presentation.
Minimalist editorial design, white background, precise alignment, consistent spacing, strong grid system.
Subtle divider lines, ultra-thin, low-contrast, neutral tone, strictly aligned to the grid.
Each panel is presented as a clean modular card with a clear hierarchy: image frame on top, minimal text block below.
Refined modern sans-serif typography, light to regular weight, tight typographic control, consistent scale rhythm.
Visual consistency across all panels: a white flying dragon and a short blond-haired young male wearing a loose flowing white robe riding on its back. A glowing spherical object remains consistent in appearance.
Style: live-action cinematic realism, human actor proportions, natural skin detail, physically accurate lighting, real-world materials, high-end film still quality, ultra high resolution, sharp focus.

Scene 06

Wide shot, upward motion
Narrative
Dragon ascends sharply as ruins collapse below, structures sinking and closing, dust fills air
Scene 08

Wide shot, high-angle view
Narrative
Dragon exits above collapsed ruins, open sky, clear silhouette of rider and dragon, resolution moment

In the distance stands a stone-built castle, while dragons circle in the sky, adding scale and atmosphere. The UI uses a glassmorphism style with semi-transparent panels, rounded corners, soft shadows, and subtle glowing edges, with slight parallax based on head movement and naturally integrated into the environment. The visuals are highly realistic with rich detail and sharp clarity, colors are natural and vibrant with slightly enhanced saturation, the air is clean with no haze or fog, and distant scenery remains crisp and clear.

text:
Use the generated image as the first-frame reference to generate the video with a Seedance 2 prompt:

Subject:
First-person player POV, with two gloved hands visible in frame, operating and adjusting a wingsuit-style flight rig attached to the body.

Style:
Live-action cinematic realism with sharp, clear details, high-speed motion that remains readable, physically accurate movement, and naturally controlled camera inertia.

Environment:
A vast sky environment with a large reflective water surface below, surrounded by cliffs and floating structures, with flying dragons in the distance.
A medieval stone castle on the ground serves as the final waypoint.
Natural daylight, clear air, high visibility, realistic water reflections, and believable spatial scale.

Mood:
A high-speed, high-tension flight experience.
Overall immersive, realistic, intense, but always clear and readable.

Timeline:
0:00 - 0:03
Start in first-person POV from high altitude. The castle waypoint is visible far ahead and shown on the mini-map.
The player dives rapidly toward the water.
Speed increases quickly, altitude drops fast, and the speed bar gradually fills.
The water surface expands rapidly in view, increasing pressure and tension.
SFX: strong wind rush
0:03 - 0:05
At the last moment before reaching the water, the player pulls up sharply, creating strong camera inertia compression.
The player skims just above the water without touching it.
Speed decreases, altitude rises, and the speed bar shortens accordingly.
Water reflections sweep quickly beneath the frame.
SFX: wind shift, surface rush
0:05 - 0:08
Transition into stable forward flight.
Movement is smooth with slight natural head motion.
The player precisely flies past cliffs and low structures.
Overall motion remains controlled and fluid.
SFX: steady airflow
0:08 - 0:11
A massive flying dragon charges directly toward the player at high speed.
The player attempts to evade but gets clipped.
The impact triggers a spin, with sky and ground flipping around the frame.
SFX: dragon roar, air blast, turbulent wind

0:11 - 0:13
The spin slows and control is gradually regained.
The horizon stabilizes.
The player re-aligns toward the castle waypoint.
Distance decreases rapidly.
SFX: wind calming
0:13 - 0:15
The player descends near the castle and reaches the waypoint, distance reaching zero.
A soft landing brings the motion to a complete stop.
After landing, the player removes the wingsuit from the body and lowers it out of view.
The camera remains steady at ground level facing the castle.
SFX: landing impact, gear removal, wind fading

text:
A public interest litigation (PIL) petition has been filed in the Supreme Court seeking legal action against the activities of the self-styled political entity “Cockroach Janta Party” for the alleged misuse and commercial exploitation of verbal observations made by the Supreme Court. 

The plea also seeks an independent probe, preferably by the CBI, into allegations concerning “fake advocates” holding fraudulent degrees who exist within legal practice.

“The emergence of entities and symbolic campaigns allegedly using courtroom expressions for publicity, trade applications, commercial engagement, and digital mobilisation demonstrates dangerous commodification of constitutional proceedings”, the plea states.

text:
#WATCH | Delhi: On energy security, Former Marketing Director, Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd., Sukhmal Kumar Jain says, "... In India, about 30 to 35% of our energy requirement is met by the oil and gas sector.
More than 50% comes from coal, and the rest is supplied by renewables, biofuels, and other sources. Looking at coal, we are doing well. Renewables have progressed, and biofuels and other alternatives have also shown good results. But since one-third of our energy requirement comes from oil and gas, this sector remains critical. In recent years, we have diversified our sourcing, which was earlier largely dependent on the Middle East. This diversification has helped. Even when the Strait of Hormuz faced restrictions and constraints, we were still able to manage. Refineries have been receiving sufficient crude, and in the last 85 days, there has been no report of cuts. Initially, there were constraints on LPG, but those have been managed well, and the situation is improving. On the LNG side, there was some impact, but since imports are only around 50%, and supplies continued from Australia, the US, and other countries, the situation remained manageable. Overall, we have diversified enough to meet our energy requirements. So, we should not worry about sourcing. The only challenge is the cost of energy. If we are efficient enough to utilise this energy in our development process, I think we will do great."
label: low

text:
Delhi | A murder case has been registered at the Paschim Vihar West police station after a 60-year-old Bijender was found dead at around 2:52 am on May 24. According to the FIR, a PCR call was received at 4:03 am reporting that a car had hit a pedestrian. Police arrived at the scene and found Bijender lying on the road in critical condition. He was taken to Sanjay Gandhi Hospital, where doctors declared him dead.

During the investigation, footage from nearby CCTV cameras was examined. A suspicious black car was visible in the footage. According to the police, at around 2:52 am, a man got out of the car, approached the victim with a stick in his hand, and attacked him several times. The accused then got back into the car and allegedly drove towards the victim twice, intending to kill him.

The FIR also states that when Bijender tried to run towards the main road to save himself, the accused chased him in the car and seriously injured him. After the incident, the accused fled in the car towards Peeragarhi village.

A case has been registered in this matter under FIR number 170/2026 at Paschim Vihar West police station under sections 103(1)/3(5) of the IPC.
label: low

text:
Delhi Police Special Cell has arrested three alleged sharpshooters linked to gangster Kapil Sangwan, along with two alleged weapon suppliers, in connection with a planned extortion firing in South-West Delhi.

According to police, the accused were allegedly sent by Kapil Sangwan alias Nandu to open fire at a clinic in the Chhawla area as part of an extortion racket being operated by the gang from abroad. The arrested accused have been identified as Abhishek, Sahil, Harshdeep alias Polu, Karan alias Ankush and Aman. Police said the three shooters were based in Punjab, while the two alleged weapon suppliers were from Haryana.

Police said three semi-automatic pistols, four live cartridges and three mobile phones used for communication with Kapil Sangwan alias Nandu were recovered from the accused. Further investigation is underway to identify other members and networks associated with the gang’s extortion operations, police said.
label: low

An average human is about 1.8 meters (6 feet) tall. 

Standing next to Starship V3 would make you look almost microscopic. 

At 124.4 meters (408 feet) tall when fully stacked, Starship V3 is the tallest and most powerful rocket ever built, standing about 69 times taller than an average person. 

It is also around 1.3 meters (4 feet) taller than the previous V2 version, giving SpaceX even more performance for future missions to the Moon, Mars, and beyond!

text:
This is the last Tesla Model S that rolled off the production line at the Tesla factory, and every employee on the floor signed the body.

The Model S launched in June 2012, a rear-wheel-drive, all-electric luxury sedan that did something no EV had done before: it made people want one. It accelerated 0–60 mph in under 2.1 seconds in Plaid specification, with a 396-mile EPA-rated range. Its interior centered on a 17-inch touchscreen at a time when most cars still had physical CD players. It won Motor Trend Car of the Year in 2013, the first time the award had gone to an electric vehicle. It proved the industry wrong about nearly everything it believed about EVs at the time.

The production line that built them is now being converted to manufacture Optimus (Tesla’s humanoid robot).

text:
Most Tesla owners don’t know this feature exists.

You can turn off the passenger-side air vent directly from the touchscreen, giving full individual control over airflow without touching any physical buttons. No manual adjustments or compromises between driver and passenger. It’s just clean digital control so each side gets exactly what it needs.

It’s a small quality-of-life detail that makes the minimalist interior work harder than it looks. Tesla replaced traditional vent controls with software, and it’s surprisingly intuitive once you find it.

Small feature. Big difference on a long drive.

Elon Musk says the biggest breakthrough in space isn’t just rockets. It’s reusable rockets.

He compares it to cars, planes, and bicycles. Imagine if every vehicle could only be used once. Transportation would be insanely expensive.

That’s exactly how space travel worked for decades.
• One launch = one destroyed rocket
• Every mission started from zero again
• Space became expensive by design
• Reusability changed the economics completely

Sometimes innovation is not about inventing something new. It’s about fixing something everyone accepted as normal.

The transition between the coffee steam and the smoky male portrait is seamless, fluid, and physically believable. The vapor portrait appears layered, three-dimensional, floating softly above the coffee cup with realistic atmospheric depth.

text:
Coding is going to be 100% done by AI by 2027

Every engineer is going to have and manage  100s of agents

The future of work is managing and building ai agents  

[Here are 10 things senior agent engineers taught me on how to make Claude Code Agents:]

→Skipping the 5 discovery questions before yapping at the agent: every run starts blank, every decision re-litigated. Blueprint → Links → Architecture → Style → Trigger, once

→Telling the agent HOW instead of the OUTCOME: caps the run at your imagination. state the north star, let the model find a path you wouldn't

→No decisions.md, no findings.md, no progress log: every cold start re-derives what last week already figured out. 5 markdown files beat 50 "as we discussed" messages

→Re-wiring Firecrawl + Apify + Gmail + Calendar into every new project: same 4 MCPs, every repo, 20 min wasted. set them at the connector layer once, forget

→Confusing the model with the IDE: Opus is the driver, Claude Code is the car. you've been tuning the car when the leverage is in the driver

→Don't keep Swapping models mid-conversation:

→Hand-tuning prompts in a feedback loop

→decisions.md as the running ledger, not your short-term memory

→skills for any flow you'd re-run more than twice

→connectors set at the user layer, not per project

→memory + loop + tools + context, written down, every project

[what actually compounds instead: ]  

→cache-aware prompt layout (static first, dynamic last, never mutate the prefix)   

→state changes via messages, never via system-prompt edits   

→GEPA / DSPy on private-data tasks, vibes on the public ones   

→managed variables on anything you'd flip without a deploy   

→outcomes (rubric + grader) for subjective quality, deterministic evals for everything else 
  
→subagents for model switching, never a mid-chat swap   

→harness discipline over prompt cleverness    

in 12 months, the gap between teams shipping on Haiku and teams burning Opus on every turn won't be talent   

it'll be harness discipline    

study this.

text:
Some really interesting insights from $NVDA's latest earnings. Admittedly have not been closely following the story but I really liked a lot of what I was hearing.

The Vera CPU is not just a part of the Rubin GPU system. It is also sold on its own. This stand-alone chip opens a completely new $200B market for NVIDIA. The company already expects nearly $20B in sales for the standalone CPU this year. Bruh...

AI is moving from answering questions to acting as independent agents. These agents act as programs that use tools like web browsers and code compilers. These tools run on CPUs, not GPUs. As billions of new agents are created, they will need a massive increase in CPUs.

NVIDIA split its data center sales into two groups. The first group is large cloud providers. The second group is called ACIE, which stands for AI clouds, industrial, and enterprise. ACIE reached $37B  and is growing fast. Jensen expects this group to grow larger than the big cloud providers over time because it covers the rest of the global economy.

Buyers no longer look at the upfront cost of a computer chip. The new goal is to get the most tokens per dollar over the life of an AI factory. This heavily favors NVIDIA because their parts work together to lower the final cost of producing intelligence.

NVIDIA is setting up computer power for Anthropic. They now partner with almost every top AI lab, including OpenAI, xAI, and $META. NVIDIA is securing its place as the foundational layer for the best AI models in the world.

NVIDIA's physical AI group made over $9B in the last year. This includes self-driving cars, factory robots, and AI-powered radio networks.

NVIDIA's LPX chip is very fast for small tasks. However, it cannot handle large data loads well. It is only meant for a small group of users with high token rates. It is not a broad threat to standard GPUs.

NVIDIA has $145B in supply agreements to keep up with demand. Even with this, Jesen expects the new Vera Rubin systems to sell out completely. They will likely face supply limits during the entire life cycle of the product.

The US government allowed NVIDIA to ship certain chips to China. NVIDIA has not made any money from this. They are not sure if China will let the chips in. They did not include any China sales in their future outlook.

And now they have some much extra cash that they are raising the dividend from a penny to $0.25 per share.

text: This is the Spotify growth thesis from their investor day that caused the stock to jump around 20%. $SPOT is focused on accelerating growth by focusing on user engagement first. Many people think streaming services restrict free content to force users into paying. Spotify actually believes this is the wrong approach. Their secret is that heavy usage on the free tier is the strongest predictor of a future paid subscription. Because of this, Spotify offers the exact same music catalog to free users to build strong daily habits. Once users are deeply engaged and love the product, Spotify charges for extra functionality like ad-free listening and offline access. This specific strategy turns massive free audiences into paying subscribers all over the world
label: low

text:
$SPOT has been absolutely pumping since its investor's day. Here are some of the best insights I found 👇

Spotify targets a mid-teens revenue CAGR through 2030. They target an operating margin of at least 20% by 2030. They target a gross margin between 35% and 40% by 2030. Long-term corporate goals include reaching 1B subscribers and reaching $100B in total revenue.

In Sweden, paid subscriber penetration is approaching 50% of the population. In the US, Spotify expanded its premium market share by 8-10% over six years. Less than 10% of Spotify users in India are currently on the Premium tier. The Premium Platinum tier in India already captures more than 7% of local subscribers. Spotify rejected the idea of the 'average user'. They are shifting to a 'power law' monetization model. Heavy users can buy specific high-margin add-on features to increase average revenue per user.

The Audiobooks+ add-on is on track to reach 100M euros in ARR this July. Podcasts achieved a second consecutive year of absolute profitability. Long-term podcast gross margins are expected to reach 40%. Users who stream video podcasts spend an extra four days per month on the app. Podcast sponsorship revenue more than doubled YoY. Spotify will launch podcast memberships this summer to let creators sell subscriptions directly to fans.

Spotify does not build or train its own general frontier LLMs. They buy core language logic on the open market. Spotify focuses its resources on training its own Large Taste Model. The Large Taste Model processes 3.4T data points every day. Early model deployments grew autoplay song saves by almost 10%. Early model deployments improved home podcast discovery by almost 10%. Early model deployments increased interactions with the AI DJ by close to 20%. Roughly 25% of premium users in the United States interact with these new AI features monthly.

AI software tools have increased engineering coding productivity by 76% since last year. More than 73% of company code contributions are now completely AI-assisted. The internal background coding agent Honk reduced developer maintenance task times by up to 90%. Spotify developers currently ship more than 4.5K functional production changes every day. Non-technical employees can now build active prototypes inside the live application codebase.

Spotify signed a landmark deal with $UMG. The deal lets users create legal AI music remixes and covers as a premium paid add-on. Original artists and songwriters will directly share the financial value of these custom remixes. Spotify launched Reserved in a multi-year deal with Live Nation. It automatically saves two concert tickets for verified top human fans before general public sales begin. Spotify uses 20 years of listening data to accurately identify these superfans. They partnered with ElevenLabs to build synthetic voice tools directly into the Spotify for Authors platform. The tool will allow independent writers to instantly generate high-quality audiobooks.

text:
$MELI hosted a webinar to help their sellers prepare for Mexico's massive Hot Sale event running from late May to early June 2026. It reveals exactly how they are shifting their business strategy to dominate the market.

Mercado Libre is temporarily slashing the fees it charges sellers to drive a massive wave of sales. For expensive items stored in their warehouses, they dropped the fee to offer interest-free payment plans from 4.5% down to just 1%. By making it much cheaper for sellers to offer financing, the platform is actively encouraging buyers to purchase big-ticket items like televisions. They are taking a hit on their profit margin per item, but they are betting that a massive spike in total sales volume will easily make up for it and increase LTV.

At the same time, they are ruthlessly optimizing their delivery network to save money. They announced that shipping costs are now based on the physical size of a box, rather than just how heavy it is. If a seller ships a small item inside a giant box, they get hit with a heavy financial penalty. On top of that, Mercado Libre is only offering free warehouse storage for top-performing items that make up the bulk of the site's sales. These strict rules force sellers to pack items tightly and clear out slow-moving inventory, which allows the company to fit significantly more packages onto every delivery truck.

To make up for the money lost on those reduced seller fees, Mercado Libre is quietly pushing merchants to buy digital ads. During the video, they admitted that the very top spots on their search page are completely reserved for paid advertisements. Since nearly all of their customers in Mexico shop on mobile phones with small screens, showing up organically is almost impossible. Sellers have to take the money they saved on retail fees and pump it directly back into Mercado Libre's ad network just to be seen. Selling digital ads is incredibly profitable, so this move protects their bottom line.

The platform is using free AI tools to lock sellers into their ecosystem. They recently rolled out an automated assistant that can instantly fix bad product photos and another tool that turns simple pictures into short, vertical videos. Creating high-quality videos and photos is usually very difficult and expensive for small business owners. By providing these advanced tools directly inside the seller dashboard for free, Mercado Libre makes it much easier to sell items while ensuring these businesses become entirely dependent on their software.
label: low

Watched this recent tour of a $MELI distribution center in Mexico City. The facility is huge, covering over 100K square meters. It holds 12M products at any given time.

During regular periods, the center ships out 200K packages daily. During busy seasons like the Hot Sale, that number jumps to 1M packages a day. At peak times, they are processing 41K packages an hour, which is about 700 packages every minute.

They grown significantly in recent years. They noted that the Mexico City center has expanded four times its size since 2019. It used to be just the ground floor, but now uses four levels.

The center supports a huge number of small businesses. Out of the 5M small and medium enterprises in Mexico, at least 1M of them sell their products through this specific warehouse. This gives a massive variety of sellers access to large-scale shipping logistics.

text:
Advertisers are worried of bad public relations. They do not want their products shown right after the wrong time or a specific event. To protect themselves, most brands use rules to block their ads from appearing on streaming news channels entirely. They throw out the baby with the bath water.

$APP's Wurl has data that proves this is a costly mistake. Over a third of news content is completely safe for brands. This includes weather, human interest stories, and financial updates. A very loyal group of consumers watch a massive amount of news. Because advertisers block the entire news category, they are completely missing this highly engaged audience.

The alpha is the ad inventory arbitrage this technology creates for AppLovin. Because the broader market blindly blocks news content, the cost to buy this ad space is artificially cheap. It is treated as 'dead' inventory by ad networks. But... Wurl's technology scans the video scene-by-scene in real time. It knows exactly when a safe story is playing right before an ad break. AppLovin can package this discarded, cheap ad space and safely sell it to major brands at a premium. Brands get access to an exclusive, captive audience they could not reach before, and AppLovin profits heavily from the spread.

When major world events happen, news viewership spikes heavily. Normally, ad networks freeze their spending during these times because the news is too unpredictable and risky. Because AppLovin can grade the content in real time, it can keep selling ads safely while its competitors are forced to sit out. This turns chaotic, unpredictable global news cycles into a highly reliable revenue engine for AppLovin.

This strategy is creating a highly profitable, cash-generating machine. In the first quarter, their gross profit grew by 40%. Because the business requires almost no physical equipment, they turn roughly 100% of their earnings directly into free cash flow. Instead of overpaying to acquire other private companies, management is returning this cash directly to its shareholders. Ethernet competes directly against older, closed network ecosystems. As AI applications move closer to end users, Arista is using its automated software and novel optical hardware to win future contracts.

Getting legal licenses, building local bank connections, and setting up payment networks in developing countries takes immense time and money. Because dLocal has already completed this difficult process across more than 60 markets, any new rival starts a decade behind. This ready-made network also delivers extreme value to dLocal's enterprise clients, which is proven by their historical NRR of over 150%. A 150% NRR means that existing customers do not just stay with the company. They spend 50% more money on the platform the following year. Global businesses trust the system so much that they continuously process more transactions and expand into new countries using dLocal's software. The infrastructure removes so much friction that merchants gladly pay higher fees each year instead of trying to build their own local payment solutions.

text:
Another great recent interview with $NU's CEO. Nubank started because the traditional banking experience was terrible. David Vélez faced huge delays, revolving doors, and extreme frustration just to open a simple bank account in Brazil. He realized that major banks were failing the consumer. To solve this, Nu focused on fighting complexity and empowering people. 

Today, this mission has turned Nu into a massive financial giant. They now have 135M customers across Brazil, Mexico, and Colombia. In the first quarter of 2026 alone, Nu reached a record $5B in revenue and $871M in net income.

From the beginning, tech gave Nu a massive edge. In 2012, the rise of smartphones and cloud computing allowed Nu to build a bank without spending billions on physical branches and old computer systems. This digital-first model makes Nu 20 to 30 times more efficient than traditional banks. This incredible efficiency resulted in a record-low operating efficiency ratio.

Now, Nu is pushing this advantage even further through AI. They are completely rebuilding the bank around it. AI now handles real-time credit pricing in under one second and has sped up engineering testing cycles by 90%.

Because Nu operates with such low costs and deep data insights, they can safely lend money in ways that others cannot. They are aggressively growing their credit card and unsecured lending businesses. These products made up 98% of their new exposure in the quarter.

Nu does not worry about minimizing late payments. Instead, they use AI to price risk extremely well. This ensures their loans remain highly profitable over the long term. They keep loan durations short so they can react to market changes much faster than incumbent banks. They are also using these advantages to capture the massive small business market at nearly zero acquisition cost.

They are quickly growing credit limits to win over high-income customers.  Nu does not view itself purely as a bank. Their true goal is to make customers love them. Because 100% of their customers are digital, Nu can easily sell non-banking products. They are now expanding into marketplaces, travel, and telecom services like NuCel to make daily life simpler.

Nu is also taking its winning formula to new countries. Their operations in Mexico and Colombia are growing very fast. They believe their Latin American culture helps them build strong emotional connections with customers. This gives them a major edge over traditional banks.

They are also testing an expansion into the US. This move is a low-cost bet that risks less than 100 basis points of their efficiency ratio but offers massive upside.  Despite their massive success, Nu is still at the very beginning of its journey. The global financial market produces around $7T in profits, and traditional banks still control 97% of it.

By combining a pro-consumer culture with top-tier AI and an unbeatable cost structure, Nu plans to keep taking market share from legacy banks. They aim to eventually serve hundreds of millions of people across many countries. It is pretty wild to see that despite their growth over the last decade that a large part of their future growth will involve winning over even more customers from legacy banks.

text:
$MELI's CFO just brought even more clarity to the Q1 results on the investor relations podcast. This team is so honest and upfront about what they are doing. Really don't know any other team like them.

The market is likely worried over a drop in profit margins for the consumer credit business in Brazil for $MELI. To some this looks like a warning sign of bad debt. Mercado Libre is experiencing accounting effects from strategic growth. They accelerated loan originations by 25% QoQ in the first quarter. Accounting rules require them to record all estimated future losses on day one before the revenue arrives. They also extended loan terms from 5 to 8 months for their best borrowers. This is just for the consumer loans. This has nothing to do with the credit cards. They also lowered interest rates to activate safe users who had never used their credit lines. The actual bad debt levels, or non-performing loans, remain stable. The margin compression is a temporary accounting reality of rapid growth, not a fundamental credit problem. The key message from the perspectives is that MELI is not seeking out riskier folks to offer lower spreads. They are reaching out to folks they deemed worthy of credit in the past and did not bite. The lower spreads along with the high duration are there to entice those that did not bite in the past.

Investors often worry when a company lowers prices or gives away shipping. Mercado Libre lowered its free shipping minimum from 79 reais to 19 reais last year in Brazil. On the surface, this looks like a direct hit to profit margins. Instead, it created immense scale. The growth rate of items sold jumped from 26% to 56%. Because the company is shipping significantly more items, their cost to ship each individual unit dropped by 17%. The profitability of cheaper items actually improved. The company traded a small amount of short-term margin for massive volume, which permanently lowered their fixed fulfillment costs per item.

Traditional banks in Argentina are seeing high default rates. Mercado Libre is seeing the exact opposite. Their non-performing loans in Argentina are stable YoY and improved in the first quarter during a time of wide-spread credit stress. Their NIMAL improved by 6%. This happens because users treat Mercado Pago as their primary financial tool. People protect their Mercado Libre credit line and prioritize paying it back over other debts. The company also purposely slowed down loan growth in Argentina to 13% to maintain this high portfolio quality. They demonstrate a structural collection advantage over traditional banks in difficult macroeconomic environments.

MELI is also spending heavily to issue new credit cards in Brazil, Mexico, and Argentina. The market often views credit card expansion as an expensive, high-risk customer acquisition cost. But folks miss that it is an ecosystem multiplier. The oldest groups of credit card users in Brazil are already profitable. The credit card users in Mexico are performing even better than Brazil after two years! Once a user gets a Mercado Pago credit card, they buy more items on the e-commerce marketplace. They also use more fintech products. The initial investment to issue the card creates a much more permanent, highly engaged user across both the commerce and financial halves of the business.

Martin also confirmed that both the cross-border business and the 1P operations are currently unprofitable and acting as a drag on overall profit margins. The market may view these depressed margins as a permanent structural flaw or a sign of inefficiency. However, he noted they are seeing clear progress and explicitly stated both segments will eventually become profitable. As the 1P and cross-border divisions continue to scale and cross their break-even points, they will transition from actively hurting margins to potentially expanding them, creating a built-in catalyst for future profitability without requiring broader cost-cutting measures. The other aspect is that as cross-border operations and 1P operations scale they will have a very similar multiplier effect like the credit cards. In fact, they could likely remain a loss-leader forever and probably still have a net-positive effect on the entire ecosystem. That is largely MELI's biggest structural strength. It is optimizing the ecosystem rather than any particular segment of operations.
label: high

text:
The market is definitely missing how $NU is funding and protecting its massive growth. Nu restructured its global operations to drop its corporate tax rate to under 9%. They are using this massive tax savings to pay for new AI tech and foreign expansion without hurting their bottom line. Their $1.79B reserve for bad loans is highly misleading. Accounting rules force them to set aside cash based on total credit limits. This means they are hoarding reserves for money that customers have not even borrowed yet.

At the same time, Nu is quietly capturing Brazil's wealthiest users by using AI to safely approve massive credit limits (these will hit profit margins but actually improve their risk profile). They are also using new public payroll loans as a a way to give Nu direct access to a user's exact salary data, stripping away the last major advantage held by traditional banks. 

Their U.S. expansion is practically risk-free. Management placed a strict, hard cap on U.S. spending. If the entire U.S. project fails, the financial loss is strictly contained and cannot damage their highly profitable Latin American core.

I feel this call gave a lot of clarity on the strategy we have seen unfolding the last few months and I feel even better about the future

text:
Thoughts on the $BN call.

The hidden value in $BN's real estate portfolio is its replacement cost economics. Management noted that building their Manhattan West complex today would cost roughly $2.5K per square foot, compared to their actual build cost of just over $1K . Because market rents would need to essentially double to justify anyone building new competing towers, their existing premium assets are protected by a massive, invisible barrier to entry.

While the market focuses on their acquisition of Just Group in the U.K., the raw organic growth is hiding in U.S. retail banking. Currently, Brookfield only sells about 1/3 of its annuities through bank channels, whereas its competitors sell roughly two-thirds. They just unlocked access to two major bank platforms this quarter and expect a third soon, paving the way for a massive, untapped wave of retail inflows.

As the front-end of the yield curve drops, day-one cash yields on new annuities suffer across the industry. While competitors get squeezed, Brookfield bypasses this trap by rotating that cash into their own proprietary, long-duration real asset and credit strategies. This allows them to maintain mid-teens returns on equity regardless of short-term rate movements. They have the unique structural advantage to shift this capital instantly between U.S. and U.K. markets based on where returns are highest, entirely free from client conflicts.

When the broader market panicked over software exposure in private credit, alternative asset managers were punished. Because Brookfield knew its software exposure was essentially zero, $BAM aggressively bought back its own stock to front-run the market's eventual realization. $BN is executing the exact same playbook, using the market's temporary dislocation to buy back its shares at an approximate 40% discount to its intrinsic value.

text:
Some insights from the $DLO earnings call

Management previously blamed high costs purely on planned 2025 investments. However, the CFO admitted on the call that Q1 expenses came in higher than expected due to "discretionary categories," "third party spend," and "slightly higher average salaries". To stop the bleeding, they are enacting a strict hiring freeze and do not expect any net new hiring for the rest of 2026.  

Brazil's gross profit dropped 20% sequentially. Management blamed normal seasonality, but also revealed a structural shift. Consumers are increasingly using the real-time payment network Pix instead of credit cards. Pix has lower monetization rates than cards. If Pix adoption continues to cannibalize card volume, Brazil's take-rates will remain under pressure.  

The brutal 77% drop in Adjusted Free Cash Flow is tied to a specific financial structure. dLocal uses a Special Purpose Vehicle to fund short-term merchant advances in Argentina. This structure currently drains their working capital. Management claims these funds will be released from the SPV and reverse the cash hit over the next three quarters.

dLocal finally closed an acquisition in Africa. However, Pedro admitted regulatory hurdles dragged the process out so long that the deal "mutated" into a simple asset purchase. The delay actually hurt their top line, and the company expects zero material revenue impact from this deal in the near term.  

dLocal is quietly moving into the physical POS business, which involves physical hardware and logistics. They are building a physical "card present" solution for one massive global client. The rollout will start in Latin America in the second half of this year. The client's contract is funding the entire build. 

dLocal officially launched a "Stable Full" stablecoin solution a few weeks ago. Pedro stated they are already seeing a "rapid increase" in merchants actively choosing to settle their funds using stablecoins.

text:
Partnering with other marketplaces is cheaper than paying for digital ads. It is often more cost-effective to pay a marketplace fee than to pay high costs for search engines and social media ads. These partnerships also did not steal sales from Casas Bahia's own website. Instead, they brought in new customers and extra website traffic. Casas Bahia is using its strong delivery network to handle the shipping for these sales.

$MELI is the clear leader in Brazil for selling home appliances and electronics. The first quarter sales were very strong and dominated by this platform. Mercado Libre also used discount coupons to help drive more sales.

Mercado Libre provides immediate, massive scale. For Casas Bahia, it is a reliable engine for high sales volume. For Mercado Libre, partnering with Casas Bahia secures a massive supply of heavy and bulky products that are hard to manage.

$SE's Shopee started by selling only cheap, low-ticket items. Now, they are aggressively expanding into home appliances and technology. Shopee wants to become a major player in these categories very quickly. They beat their December growth targets and are using heavy discount coupons to win customers.

Shopee is an aggressive challenger that brings fast growth. For Casas Bahia, it opens up a new audience that loves discounts. For Shopee, it proves they can successfully sell expensive items, not just cheap goods.

The $AMZN partnership is still very new and in the early connection phase. Casas Bahia is still learning how Amazon's pricing and seller tools work. Amazon has big goals for Brazil and wants to grow steadily over a long period.

Amazon is a long-term play. It requires a learning curve to figure out the technical and pricing differences. For Casas Bahia, it is a steady, sustainable sales channel. For Amazon, having Casas Bahia's products helps them compete better against Mercado Libre in Brazil.

text:
$NU is no longer just a bank for the mass market. They are aggressively capturing Brazil's wealthiest citizens. Right now, two out of every five high-income Brazilians are Nu customers. Three out of every five "super core" customers, the middle-to-upper tier, already use the platform. Historically, Nu struggled with this demographic because their old models would not grant high credit limits to rich users. Nu is now using new AI underwriting models to aggressively increase credit limits for the wealthy. Because of this fix, high-income purchase volume is now surging by more than 40%.

The market views private payroll loans simply as a safe way to lend money. Nu views them as a data extraction tool. Currently, legacy top-five banks control the payroll agreements for large corporations. Because of this, Nu has historically been blind to specific employee data. When Nu eventually scales its own private payroll product, it will gain exact access to a customer's real salary, their time at the company, and their expected severance pay. This effectively closes the final data advantage held by traditional legacy banks.

Nu is actively ignoring two massive government tailwinds in its risk modeling. Brazil enacted a new income tax exemption for workers making up to 5K reais per month. This directly increases the disposable cash of Nu's core customer base. Also a new federal government debt renegotiation program is launching later this year. Management confirmed they did not factor either of these positive events into their current bad loan reserves. If these government programs succeed, Nu is currently over-reserving for bad loans, meaning future profits could be artificially compressed right now.

Despite regulatory setbacks regarding FGTS loans, Nu's secured lending portfolio still grew 38%. Management explained why the regulatory hit will not ruin their balance sheet. These specific FGTS loans have a long duration of more than 36 months. This means any drop in new loan creation takes a very long time to actually hurt total balance sheet volume. In the meantime, Nu is actively replacing that lost volume by originating new public payroll loans instead.
label: low

text:
$NU has quietly built the largest SME customer base in Brazil. They now have over 5M SME accounts. They built this base with a customer acquisition cost of nearly zero. They achieved this by cross-selling to their existing 110M individual users who also run small businesses. Now, they are pushing new secured and unsecured credit lines to these businesses and moving upmarket to target companies with 10 to 15 employees. Management views this as a massive, unpriced advantage.

Analysts questioned why Nu heavily increased their cash provisions for bad loans. Management clarified that this is not a prediction of a crashing economy. They explicitly stated that their underwriting models always assume the future will be worse than the past. They also keep their loan durations intentionally shorter than the market average. This short duration allows them to pivot immediately if loans start failing. Their internal models do not even account for two massive upcoming tailwinds of a new Brazilian income tax exemption for workers making up to 7.4K reais a month, and a new government debt renegotiation program.

Competitors are aggressively pushing private payroll loans because they are supposed to be safe and secured. Nu is intentionally moving slow in this market. They found hidden risks in the system, noting an incredibly high 10% to 15% first-payment default rate for a supposedly secure product. Nu is also refusing to charge high interest rates on these loans because they expect the government will eventually cap the rates. They are letting their competitors absorb the regulatory risk while they wait.

Nu posted a record-low efficiency ratio of 17.6% in the first quarter. Investors should not expect this exact metric to last. Management admitted that roughly two-thirds of this beat was just caused by timing. They pushed certain real estate and marketing expenses into later quarters. The true efficiency ratio for the full year 2026 will rise back up to roughly 20%. However, the remaining one-third of the efficiency gain is structural and permanent, driven directly by replacing human operations with AI. 👏

text:
The market may see the rising bad loans and the shrinking profit margins and get nervous. That is the wrong way to read this $NU report.

Bad loans are the feature here. Nu is not accidentally making bad loans. They are doing it on purpose. Traditional banks try to minimize bad loans to protect their margins. Nu is intentionally degrading its own loan margins to capture massive market share. Just like a business we may know: $MELI.

AI is controlling the risk. Nu is using NuFormer to approve these risky loans in under one second. The AI calculates the exact price needed to ensure that the people who do pay back cover the costs of the people who default. They know defaults will rise and that is part of the strategy.

The float is paying for the expansion. Lending is getting less profitable, but Nu does not care because they are sitting on $42.4B in customer cash. They earn interest just by holding this money. This float money now makes up nearly 40% of their total gross profit.

Nu is using the massive, safe profits from holding customer cash to fund an aggressive, AI-driven expansion into riskier lending. They are trading high percentage margins for massive total dollar volume. It is a highly calculated, risk-heavy land grab and should help them postion well alongside MELI's massive on-going land grab.

I will have to go back through the transcript later.

text:
$NU reported massive top-line revenue growth, surpassing $5B for the first time, but the underlying data reveals a costly shift in their monetization engine. Nu is heavily leveraging new proprietary AI foundation models, NuFormer, to price and approve unsecured personal loans individually based on predicted NPV in under a second. This AI integration was a core driver behind a 40% YoY expansion of their total credit portfolio, which reached $37.2B . However, this rapid growth is explicitly fueled by intentional expansion into higher-risk borrower segments.

Early delinquency 15-90 day NPLs rose 89 basis points sequentially to 5%. To cover these riskier segments, their credit loss allowances surged 33% QoQ to $1.79B . The hidden negative for the quarter is that their risk-adjusted net interest margin, which is the true measure of lending profitability, fell by 100 basis points from 10.5% in Q4 2025 down to 9.5%. This lowered profitability on lending is primed to grow investor skepticism, similar to the market reactions seen with $MELI.

To balance these shrinking lending margins, Nu is relying heavily on holding cash. Elevated credit loss allowances reduced credit's relative contribution, resulting in a massive 41% of their gross profit now coming from float, which is the interest earned on customer deposits, rather than actual lending operations.

On the positive side, their international expansion is proving structurally safer than expected. Mexico officially broke even in Q1 2026, proving the core earnings formula translates abroad. In the US, Nu received conditional approval from the OCC for a national bank charter in January, but they have strictly capped their initial spending. Management confirmed that the US expansion investment will remain below 100 basis points on the consolidated efficiency ratio in both 2026 and 2027.

The real alpha is that Nu is effectively trading loan profit margins for fast AI-driven credit growth, while utilizing cash float and strict foreign cost-caps to maintain high total profitability.

This was a stellar quarter! I am very optimistic about the opportunities for $MELI and $NU in LatAm. Looking forward to the call!

$DLO is processing more money but capturing much less immediate value. While payment volume grew, actual revenue shrank because the company lost basis points on both processing and foreign exchange spreads. Large merchants are squeezing their transaction rates. The company's core foundation is also stalling. Latin America is losing momentum, dragged down by a steep drop in Brazilian profits. To compensate, dLocal is seeking growth in even less mature markets across Africa and Asia. This geographic pivot exposes them to different structural risks.

And still just ten clients generate 62% of the company's total revenue. If a single major merchant demands a price cut, dLocal's already shrinking margins will collapse further. When combined with unpredictable emerging market regulations, like their recent $9.7M surprise tax penalty, the underlying business looks increasingly volatile.

text:
Hohn’s strategy essentially redefines quality as extreme resilience against substitution and competition over a multi-decade timeline. Hohn targets super-companies where physical, non-replicable assets create monopolistic barriers to entry, rendering executive talent largely irrelevant to the underlying cash flows. He deliberately avoids high-growth sectors vulnerable to disruption, viewing terminal value as the greatest source of alpha missed by short-term analysts. 

For instance, in infrastructure, he targets assets like cell towers, rail lines, and literal toll roads where tenancy ratios allow for near-100% margin expansion on zero-cost additions, and cash flow yields are contractually linked to inflation.

In aerospace, his conviction is absolute. TCI's largest holding is $GE Aerospace. Hohn categorizes this as an unbreakable moat because the complexity of building aircraft engines has prevented any new market entrants in over 50 years. The true economic value, however, is not the engine itself, but the captive aftermarket spare parts and servicing ecosystem. Once an airline adopts an engine, the switching costs are effectively insurmountable, guaranteeing decades of highly predictable, high-margin revenue. 

Hohn’s view on moats is dynamic. He believes even the strongest moats can erode. In a definitive move in early 2026, TCI slashed a large portion of its $8B stake in $MSFT, which is a position held for nearly a decade. Hohn explicitly cited that the rapid advancement of Generative AI posed an existential risk to Microsoft's core Office and Azure moats, proving that he will mercilessly abandon a historically 'high quality' asset the moment technological disruption threatens its terminal value.

text:
🚨THE FBI CREATED A FAKE CRYPTOCURRENCY.. LISTED IT ON UNISWAP.. HIRED MARKET MAKERS TO PUMP IT.. THEN ARRESTED EVERYONE WHO SAID YES.. 

THIS IS THE CRAZIEST LAW ENFORCEMENT OPERATION IN CRYPTO HISTORY!!!

The FBI built an actual ERC-20 token on Ethereum called NexFundAI.. 100 billion token supply.. A professional website.. Whitepapers promising "passive income through AI-powered investing"..

It looked exactly like every other crypto project.. Because that was the point..

Undercover agents posed as the founding team.. Then reached out to professional market-making firms and said "we need you to fake our trading volume"..

Every single firm said yes..

Here's what they recorded..

Gotbit.. A firm run by a 26-year-old Russian who publicly bragged in 2019 that he built a business faking trade volumes.. His team kept internal spreadsheets with columns literally labeled "fake volume" vs "market volume"..

When asked how fast they could pump NexFundAI's volume to $1 million per day.. They said "6 hours.. It will cost about $200"..

$200 to fake $1 million in daily trading volume..

MyTrade.. Run by a guy who called himself "the mastermind".. He explained the exact psychology of the scam on camera..

"We make the chart look like a really nice roller coaster ride.. That's where people jump in.. We have to make them lose money in order to make profit"..

He said that on a recorded FBI video call..

CLS Global.. A Dubai-based firm.. Their bots generated 98% of NexFundAI's total trading volume.. When the FBI asked if they could sync fake volume spikes with fake news announcements.. They said absolutely..

ZM Quant.. Bots executing 10 to 20 trades per minute through dozens of wallets to look organic..

All of them knew it was fraud.. All of them did it anyway.. All of it was recorded..

And the clients were even worse..

Saitama.. A meme coin that hit $7.5 billion market cap.. The founders coordinated buys through private Telegram chats.. Sent "pump it" memes while manipulating the price.. Then dumped on retail investors..

$7.5 billion.. Built entirely on fake volume.. Every penny of real money came from retail investors who thought the momentum was organic..

One founder left Saitama and started Robo Inu.. Used Gotbit again.. Another launched VZZN.. Same playbook..

Lillian Finance.. Founder claimed to be a defense contractor who addressed Congress.. Marketed the token as funding children's hospitals.. Pocketed everything..

When the FBI shut it down.. They seized $25 million in one day.. 18 people indicted across the US, UK, and Portugal.. The CEO of Gotbit was arrested in Portugal and extradited.. Sentenced to 8 months plus $23 million forfeiture..

But here's the part that broke my brain..

Real people bought NexFundAI..

The FBI's fake token.. With zero utility.. Zero real developers.. Created solely to catch criminals.. Attracted real retail investors because the fake volume made the chart look bullish..

When the FBI pulled the liquidity to end the operation.. Those people lost real money.. On a government-issued token..

The FBI had to set up a restitution portal to pay them back..

And it gets worse..

Within 24 hours of the DOJ announcing the sting.. Someone cloned the FBI's exact smart contract.. Launched a copycat token.. Rode the viral momentum.. And made $127,000 in a single day..

Using the exact same manipulation tactics the FBI just arrested 18 people for..

Then in 2026.. The FBI did it again.. New token called Lexobit.. 10 more arrests.. Including operators extradited from Singapore..

IRS forensics showed that in one firm's trading.. 1,209 out of 1,221 consecutive transactions went straight back to wallets the firm controlled.. 99% circular..

The FBI proved what everyone in crypto suspected..

The volume is fake.. The charts are painted.. The momentum is manufactured..

And every time you buy a token because "the chart looks bullish".. You might be the exit liquidity.

text:
JUST IN: 8,000 Meta workers got fired by email this morning. Singapore got the notice at 4 AM. The moment the email arrived, their laptops, work email, and accounts went dark. That is how a $1.7 trillion company tells you it is over.

Inside Meta, this round has a name. They are calling it "Pralaydin," which is Hindi for "day of catastrophe." Reuters reports this is the third firing round of 2026, and the full year could end with 22,000 people gone. About one in five Meta employees.

Analysts at Evercore say firing these 8,000 people will save Meta around $3 billion a year. That sounds like a lot. Zuckerberg has been writing much bigger checks for AI hires. Last summer he paid an ex-Apple engineer named Ruoming Pang $200 million to come work on AI. He paid another AI researcher, Andrew Tulloch, a reported $1.5 billion over six years. He wrote a $14.3 billion check just to get the CEO of an AI startup called Scale to join the team.

The same week Meta laid off 8,000 office workers, the company is still planning to spend somewhere between $125 and $145 billion this year on AI data centers and the chips to run them. The firings save about 2% of that.

Two days before the layoffs began, Meta picked 7,000 of the survivors and moved them onto brand new AI teams. The people getting hit in this round are mostly engineers and product managers. Another wave is already planned for later this year.

Meta is not in trouble. Far from it. The company made $56 billion in revenue in just the first three months of 2026. That was up 33% from a year ago. Pure profit in those same three months was nearly $27 billion. The stock is off its September high but Meta is still worth $1.7 trillion.

The severance is good on paper. 16 weeks of base pay, plus two extra weeks for every year you put in, plus a year and a half of health insurance. The average payout works out to about $360,000 per person.

The money does arrive. But finding a new job is brutal. Engineers who got cut in earlier Meta rounds say they have sent out hundreds of job applications and heard back from almost nobody, even with Meta on the resume.

So a wildly profitable company is firing 8,000 office workers via 4 AM email and instant lockout, while writing checks worth hundreds of millions of dollars to a small group of new AI hires. The Singapore workers reading their termination email at 4 AM were paying for the next $200 million hire.

text:
Greg Isenberg broke down a 35-step playbook for building AI startups from zero to $1M+/year. 4 businesses, all profitable, no VC money

I compressed it into 12 rules that actually matter:

1. ride a trend, don't create one: find a niche where demand already exists but tools are 5 years behind. if people are complaining in reddit threads and facebook groups.. that's your market

2. audience before product: master ONE platform. set notifications for 10 niche leaders, reply with real insight (not "great post!"), gain 5-10 followers daily. this compounds into an unfair advantage when you launch

3. validate with wallets, not surveys: if nobody will pre-pay at 50-70% off for something that doesn't exist yet.. they won't pay full price either. your audience becomes your investors

4. vibe code the MVP: use Cursor/Bolt/V0 to ship v1 in days not months. the goal isn't perfect code, it's proving people will use it. polish comes after revenue

5. keep the team at zero: AI is your co-founder now. Manus for research, ChatGPT for PRDs, Claude Design for design, Cursor for code. most $1M/yr businesses in 2026 run with 1-3 people max

6. automate before you hire: find the 3-step process you do 10x a day that takes 5 minutes each time. automate that first. Lindy, Gumloop, Zapier. work toward 90%+ automation

7. retention before growth: fixing churn is 10x more valuable than doubling acquisition. run retention sprints before growth sprints. most founders do this backwards and wonder why revenue flatlines

8. modular pricing kills one-size-fits-all: free tier for top of funnel, $29 for individuals, $299 for teams, $3K for enterprise. let the product sell itself at every level

9. partner with creators instead of buying ads: offer 1-20% equity or 20-50% rev share to creators with your audience. one partnership can outperform 6 months of paid marketing

10. build free tools for distribution: a public-facing calculator, checker, or generator drives organic traffic AND trains AI search to recommend you. this is the new SEO

11. think portfolio, not single product: once business #1 is profitable, repeat the process. share infrastructure, cross-promote, create a flywheel. Walt Disney didn't build one ride

12. ship something new every 30 days: a culture of shipping beats a culture of planning. new MVP monthly, acquire underperforming products with distribution upside, recruit operators to run them

what actually compounds in 2026:

- audience before product, not the other way
- pre-selling before building
- 1-person teams running $1M businesses with AI
- retention over acquisition, always
- portfolios over single bets
- shipping over strategizing

the cost of building has never been lower.. billions of people with credit cards are reachable through social media

the only bottleneck left is you actually starting

full 30-min breakdown from @gregisenberg attached below ↓

study this 

P.S. left 20 not taken startup ideas below which you can take and start growing

The 20 BIGGEST startup ideas I'd build if I had 20 lives

1. biggest subscriptions: kill agent. average person bleeds $219/month on charges they forgot about. connect bank, show usage, cancel in one click. you keep 20% of what you save them

2. biggest insurance: AI that fights denied claims for you. insurers reject 30-40% on first try on purpose hoping you give up. most people do

3. biggest death tech: digital afterlife manager. you have 200+ accounts. crypto, subscriptions, passwords. when you die, half of it just.. disappears. 48% of americans have zero plan for this

4. biggest negotiation: AI that handles price negotiations over email. car deals, medical bills, salary offers. most people leave $5k-$50k/year on the table because they hate the back and forth

5. biggest fintech: AI tax strategist. not filing, strategy. what your $2k/hour CPA knows but packaged for $99/month

6. biggest debt: AI settlement negotiator. americans owe $1.1T in credit card debt. collection agencies buy your debt for pennies on the dollar and then charge you full price. AI that negotiates settlements at 30-50 cents on the dollar

7. biggest manufacturing: micro-factory OS. 3D printers went from $100k to $200 but the software stayed at $50k. thousands of people making real products from garages with spreadsheets

8. biggest fraud prevention: B2B payment verification. 76% of companies got hit last year. $133k average per incident. AI that checks every outgoing payment before it leaves

9. biggest construction: permit automation. you're waiting 3-6 months for paperwork that AI can fill in minutes. 1.5M permits/year in the US

10. biggest government: AI benefits navigator. $140B in federal benefits go unclaimed every year. people qualify but can't get through the paperwork. 47 pages to apply for programs that exist to help you

11. biggest local biz: AI reputation manager. one 1-star google review can kill a small business overnight. monitor everything, auto-respond, push positive results up. most owners have no idea what's being said about them

12. biggest freelance: AI contract reviewer. 70M+ freelancers signing stuff they don't fully read. flag risks in 30 seconds, charge $19/month

13. biggest field work: voice CRM. electricians and plumbers don't sit at desks. they need a CRM they can talk to while driving between jobs. 60M+ workers, nothing good exists

14. biggest proptech: phone-based property inspector. point at a room, get a full report. the $5B inspection industry still literally uses clipboards

15. biggest divorce: separation logistics AI. 750k divorces per year in the US. asset splitting, custody scheduling, document filing. lawyers charge $15-30k for what's mostly paperwork and coordination

16. biggest renovation: AI cost estimator. homeowners always get 3-5 completely different quotes from contractors. scan your rooms, know the real number before calling anyone

17. biggest compliance: EU e-invoicing tools. 10M+ SMBs are forced to go digital by 2027 and have no clue how. $22B market btw

18. biggest immigration: visa application automation. people spend $5-15k on immigration lawyers for paperwork that follows a process. millions of applications per year, all manually filled

19. biggest legal: AI dispute resolver. small claims under $10k take months in court and cost more in lawyer fees than the claim itself. online AI mediation that settles it in days for a flat fee

20. biggest healthcare: AI second opinion. 12M americans get misdiagnosed every year. 795,000 die or get permanently disabled from diagnostic errors. upload your labs, get a second analysis before making a life-changing decision
